sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at [20,20]. Let me check for red objects and try to find chambers along the red path.
def find_player(seg):
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24 and n['children']:
            return n
    return None

p = find_player(current_frame.segmentation)
print(f"Player: {p['boundary'][0] if p else '?'}")

# Check for red objects
red_objects = [n for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R']
print(f"Red objects: {len(red_objects)}")
for r in red_objects:
    print(f"  pixels={r['pixels']}, boundary={r['boundary']}")

# Try going RIGHT from [8,38] to see if there's a chamber at [8,44]
# First go UP, UP to get back to top row, then RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT to [8,38], then RIGHT
action(['UP', 'UP', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

White path forms loop. Red path on right side. Target at [49,43]. </parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
